data.description:
Pre-existing failure surfaced during full-suite verification on 2026-06-10 (NOT introduced by the 10-ticket batch; reproduces at commit 39dbfba, before any of that work; the rebar_reconciler/ engine is untouched by it).

## Symptoms
tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_once.py — 3 failures:
- test_idempotency_two_passes_with_unchanged_remote: second reconcile_once() over an UNCHANGED remote returns mutation_count=2, expected 0 (idempotency broken or prev-snapshot not read back).
- test_excluded_fields_change_does_not_drive_mutations
- test_real_field_change_converges_after_one_pass

Accompanied by a logged ERROR: "reconcile: binding-store commit to tickets branch failed (CalledProcessError(128, [git, -C, <tmp>/.tickets-tracker, add, .bridge_state/bindings.json]))" — the temp tracker git add exits 128, so bindings persist to filesystem only and the prev-snapshot the second pass should read may be missing/mismatched.

## Repro
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_once.py -q  (3 failed)
Also fails at 39dbfba in a clean worktree -> pre-existing.

## Likely area
reconcile_once prev-snapshot / binding-store persistence path; the git-add-128 on the temp tracker suggests the binding-store commit step assumes a tracker git state the test fixture does not set up (or a real persistence bug).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root-cause the git add exit-128 in the binding-store commit path under the test fixture
- [ ] Second pass over unchanged remote yields mutation_count=0 (idempotency) — RED test already exists
- [ ] All three test_reconcile_once.py tests green
- [ ] No regression to live reconcile binding persistence
data.title: test_reconcile_once: 2nd pass over unchanged remote yields mutation_count=2 (not 0); binding-store git add fails (exit 128)
